reforms.description: A new standards-based apprenticeship system was introduced in September 1993, replacing the traditional apprenticeship model with compulsory standards-based training and education, culminating in a National Craft Certificate. Additionally, the National Training Agency has undertaken a major overhaul of craft training, replacing traditional apprenticeships with modular courses that combine theoretical instruction and practical experience.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, which is essential for improving productivity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The new standards-based apprenticeship system was officially introduced in September 1993.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a new apprenticeship system and overhaul of craft training represents a significant structural change in vocational education.
reforms.source_quote: A new standards-based apprenticeship system was introduced in September 1993 and will be generalised by September 1995.

reforms.description: The government has planned reforms to improve the quality of education at the primary and secondary levels, including the introduction of a new science course and changes to teaching methods to ensure specific learning objectives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education quality is crucial for developing a skilled workforce, which in turn supports innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans for reforms in education.
reforms.importance_rationale: The planned reforms represent a significant overhaul of the education system aimed at enhancing learning outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The government has already planned reforms to improve the quality of education at the primary and secondary levels.

reforms.description: The Operational Programme for Industrial Development 1994/99 was launched to enhance the competitive capability of indigenous firms, aiming for long-term self-sustaining employment creation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the competitive capability of firms, the programme is expected to foster innovation and create sustainable jobs.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was officially launched in 1994.
reforms.importance_rationale: The launch of a comprehensive programme aimed at industrial development represents a significant policy shift.
reforms.source_quote: The Operational Programme for Industrial Development 1994/99 is launched. The primary focus of the Operational Programme is to build up the competitive capability of indigenous firms.

reforms.description: The Community Employment Programme (CEP) was introduced to replace existing employment schemes, significantly increasing participant numbers and providing training modules aimed at improving job prospects for the long-term unemployed.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing training and improving job prospects, the programme supports workforce development and economic participation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was introduced to replace existing employment schemes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the CEP represents a significant but narrower adjustment to existing employment schemes.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduces a major new employment initiative, the Community Employment Programme (CEP).
